events.label: assistant
events.text:
Wrote the assessment to `/app/answer.json`.

Summary: the reverse-lapped WRB (upper course behind lower) inverts the drainage plane, directing water behind the barrier at every lap line instead of shedding it outward — a clear violation of IRC R703 and manufacturer shingle-lap requirements. Decision is **fail**, risk **high**. Work must stop in the affected area, the defective courses cut back and reinstalled with correct shingle-fashion overlap, dependent flashing re-integrated into the corrected plane, and the area re-inspected before cladding or further flashing work proceeds.
